title: How a three-way merge computes the result
description: A merge combines two tips relative to their common ancestor, and a conflict arises only when both sides changed the same region differently.
body: Use when reasoning about why a merge conflicted or produced a given result. Do not assume conflicts come from any overlapping edit. Git compares each side to the merge base: a region changed on only one side applies cleanly, while the same region changed differently on both sides becomes a conflict. Inspect the base with git merge-base to understand a confusing conflict. Validation signals include a merge result that matches a manual three-way reading of base, ours, and theirs. Risk boundary: rename and move detection can mislabel hunks, so verify the merged file rather than trusting auto-resolution on heavily moved code.
